---
title: "OpenStreetMap"
engine: julia
---

```{julia}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
using GeoDataAccess
using GeoDataAccess: DataAccessPlan, fetch, MetaData, OpenStreetMap
using Dates, JSON
using GeoInterface.Extents: Extent
```

[OpenStreetMap](https://www.openstreetmap.org/) is a collaborative, open-source map of the
world.  GeoDataAccess queries OSM data via the
[Overpass API](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Overpass_API), which supports bounding box
queries filtered by feature type (tag key).  No API key is required.

```{julia}
MetaData(OpenStreetMap())
```

## Basic Usage

Each "variable" corresponds to an OSM tag key.  One API request is made per variable.

```{julia}
# Buildings near Times Square
plan = DataAccessPlan(OpenStreetMap(), (-73.9857, 40.7580);
    variables = [:building])
```

```{julia}
files = fetch(plan)
data = JSON.parsefile(files[1])
println("Elements returned: ", length(data["elements"]))
```

## Extent Query

Use an `Extent` to query a bounding box.  Keep areas small to avoid timeouts.

```{julia}
ext = Extent(X=(-73.99, -73.98), Y=(40.74, 40.75))

plan = DataAccessPlan(OpenStreetMap(), ext;
    variables = [:highway, :amenity],
    timeout = 60)
```

```{julia}
files = fetch(plan)
for (var, file) in zip([:highway, :amenity], files)
    n = length(JSON.parsefile(file)["elements"])
    println("$var: $n elements")
end
```

## Options

| Keyword | Type | Default | Description |
|---------|------|---------|-------------|
| `variables` | `Vector{Symbol}` | `[:building]` | OSM tag keys to query |
| `timeout` | `Int` | `90` | Overpass query timeout (seconds) |

## Variables

Each variable is an [OSM tag key](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Map_features).
The API returns all elements that have the specified tag, regardless of its value.

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `building` | Buildings (residential, commercial, etc.) |
| `highway` | Roads, paths, and streets |
| `natural` | Natural features (water, wood, etc.) |
| `amenity` | Amenities (restaurants, schools, hospitals, etc.) |
| `shop` | Shops and retail |
| `leisure` | Leisure areas (parks, playgrounds, etc.) |
| `landuse` | Land use zones (residential, industrial, etc.) |
| `waterway` | Waterways (rivers, streams, canals) |
| `railway` | Railway infrastructure |
| `boundary` | Administrative boundaries |
| `tourism` | Tourism features (hotels, attractions, etc.) |
| `power` | Power infrastructure (lines, substations, etc.) |

::: {.callout-tip}
These are the most common tag keys, but any valid OSM tag key can be used as a variable.
:::

## Loading as a DataFrame

Install `DataFrames` to enable the `load` function, which parses Overpass JSON into a
tabular format:

```julia
using Pkg
Pkg.add("DataFrames")

using DataFrames
df = load(plan)
```

The resulting DataFrame has columns:

| Column | Type | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| `osm_type` | `String` | Element type: `"node"`, `"way"`, or `"relation"` |
| `osm_id` | `Int` | OpenStreetMap element ID |
| `latitude` | `Float64` | Center latitude |
| `longitude` | `Float64` | Center longitude |
| `tag_key` | `String` | The queried tag key |
| `tag_value` | `String` | Value of the queried tag (e.g., `"yes"`, `"residential"`) |
| `name` | `String` | Element name (if available, otherwise `missing`) |
| `tags` | `String` | All tags as a JSON string |

## Tips

- **Keep bounding boxes small.** Dense urban areas can return hundreds of thousands of
  elements.  Start with a small area and expand as needed.
- **Point queries** use a ~1 km buffer (0.005Â° in each direction).
- **Rate limits** are informal.  Avoid hammering the Overpass API with rapid sequential
  requests.
- **Caching** works as with all other sources â€” repeated queries hit the local cache.
- **License**: OSM data is licensed under the
  [Open Data Commons Open Database License](https://opendatacommons.org/licenses/odbl/) (ODbL 1.0).
